entries.content: Real investment in fixed nonresidential business capital (equipment and structures) constructed from two spliced sources: (1) BEA Fixed Reproducible Tangible Wealth of the United States (1977, table B4, pp. 343-344), Investment in Fixed Nonresidential Business Capital, 1832-1975, originally in 1970=100; (2) BEA Wealth Table 4.8, line 1 (Investment in Fixed Nonresidential Capital), 1901-2010. Both series were rebased to 1958=100 and spliced at 1901 to create a continuous real investment index.

BEA Fixed Reproducible Tangible Wealth of the United States, 1925-75, Table B4, pp. 343-344. BEA Wealth Table 4.8, line 1, accessed 9/2/2011. Extension via FRED GPDIC1 (Real Gross Private Domestic Investment, billions chained 2017 dollars).

Fig 2.2 displays real U.S. investment in fixed nonresidential business capital (1958=100) from 1832 to present. The chart shows the extreme volatility of investment over the long run, including railroad booms of the 1840s-1850s, Civil War disruptions, the Great Depression collapse, and postwar investment cycles.

citations.full: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Fixed Reproducible Tangible Wealth of the United States, 1925-75. Washington, DC: U.S. Government Printing Office, 1977. Table B4, pp. 343-344.
U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Wealth Tables. Table 4.8, Line 1: Investment in Fixed Nonresidential Capital. Accessed September 2, 2011.
Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED). Real Gross Private Domestic Investment (GPDIC1). St. Louis Fed. https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/GPDIC1

methodology_summary: S002 combines BEA historical fixed investment (1832-1975, table B4) with BEA Wealth Table 4.8 line 1 (1901-2010). The earlier BEA series covers investment in fixed nonresidential business capital (equipment and structures) in constant dollars; the Wealth table provides the modern NIPA-consistent series. Both were rebased to 1958=100 and spliced at 1901. Extension uses FRED GPDIC1 (real, chained 2017 dollars) re-indexed at overlap.
